description: Sector overlay for banks, payment institutions, e-money firms, fintech platforms, lending/payment products, and related financial-services documentation in the UK.

metadata.scope: UK sector overlay controls for Banking and Fintech. Intended to be used with uk_core_control_library.
metadata.source_note: Source basis is mapped from official UK legal/regulatory sources available at rule-pack publication time. This starter pack is not legal advice and requires expert validation before production reliance.
metadata.external_knowledge_policy: No external knowledge is introduced during document evaluation. Regulatory source mappings live only in this versioned rule pack.
metadata.territorial_scope_note: UK law can differ across England, Wales, Scotland, and Northern Ireland. This starter pack uses UK-wide sources where available and flags devolved or nation-specific application for human review.

primary_sources.name: Payment Services Regulations 2017 and E-Money Regulations 2011
FCA financial crime and AML materials
PRA Rulebook and prudential supervision
primary_sources.issuer: Prudential Regulation Authority / Bank of England

rules.title: FCA/PRA authorisation or regulated-status evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for FCA authorisation, PRA authorisation, FRN, regulated status, permissions, or financial-services authorisation evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified UK compliance/legal reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, territorial scope, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.
source_basis: Financial Services and Markets Act framework

rules.title: Customer due diligence and financial-crime controls are documented
rules.summary: Checks for CDD, KYC, beneficial ownership, sanctions screening, transaction monitoring, or suspicious activity reporting evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified UK compliance/legal reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, territorial scope, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Consumer Duty outcome evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for Consumer Duty, good outcomes, fair value, consumer understanding, or customer-support evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified UK compliance/legal reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, territorial scope, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Payment services or e-money authorisation evidence is present where applicable
rules.summary: Checks for payment institution, e-money issuer, EMI, PSP, payment initiation, account information, or safeguarding evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified UK compliance/legal reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, territorial scope, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Client money/assets safeguarding evidence is present where applicable
rules.summary: Checks for CASS, client money, custody assets, segregation, reconciliation, or client-assets return evidence.
